stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 5 observations with mean 78.2 % (within-factory SD = 7.0). factory B has 10 observations with mean 70.4 % (within-factory SD = 3.8). factory C has 20 observations with mean 65.6 % (within-factory SD = 7.0). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.4 and 18.5 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 77.6 % (shrunk from raw 78.2). Range [76.8, 77.9] as between-factory SD varies from 7.4 to 18.5. Pooled mean: 68.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 68.8 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=12.3: B = 0.939.
Interval [76.8, 77.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (78.2) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (68.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
